info.title: Device Management API
info.description: API with WebSocket support for device management, E2B sandboxes, Knowledge Base and Safety

post.description:
Initiate password reset flow.
Always returns 202 to prevent email enumeration.
Reset password using a valid reset token.

get.description: Initiate Google OAuth2 flow. Redirects to Google's authorization page.
Handle the Google OAuth2 callback.

patch.description:
Accept GA/UTM meta collected client-side before Google OAuth redirect.
Merges into user.registration_meta and re-tracks registration_completed.
Called once from GoogleCallbackPage for new users only.

post.description:
Mobile (Android / iOS) Google authentication.

The client verifies the user with Google on-device and sends the resulting
ID token here. We validate it, then find-or-create the user and return a
JWT access token. No redirect flow needed for mobile.

post.description:
Force-release all locks and bindings for a device.

Clears device_lock, cloud_pc_session binding, and any active task
registration so the device can be used in a new session immediately.

Create a new chat session.

Args:
    reuse_existing: If True and an active session exists for this device,
                   return it instead of creating a new one. Default: False.

Note:
    device_id is optional. Sessions without device_id are "chat-only" sessions
    where the user can have conversations without device control capabilities.

get.description:
List user's chat sessions with pagination.
Optimized: single query with window function for count.

delete.description:
Permanently delete a chat session and all its messages.
This action is irreversible.

get.description:
Get the latest agent plan for a session.

Returns the most recent non-superseded plan payload (type=plan)
or null if no plan exists.  Used by the frontend on page reload
to restore the expandable step list.

Получить краткую историю сессии (только user queries + final responses).

Используется для UI, чтобы показать упрощенную версию истории без промежуточных шагов.

Args:
    session_id: ID сессии
    max_messages: Максимальное количество сообщений в summary (default: 10)

Returns:
    Список сообщений в упрощенном формате:
    - Все user messages
    - Только финальные assistant responses (с summary/completed status)
    - Промежуточные agent_step сообщения исключены

schema.title: Response List My Tasks Tasks Get
Response List Chat Messages Sessions  Session Id  Messages Get

post.description:
Rate an agent message (1-5 stars with optional comment).

- Only assistant messages can be rated.
- One rating per user per message (upsert).

get.description: Get the current user's rating for a specific message.

post.description:
UNIFIED DEVICE ACTION EXECUTION

Automatically routes to:
- Real devices -> WebSocket
- E2B sandboxes -> E2B SDK

Agent doesn't need to know device type!

get.description: Получить результат выполнения действия (polling эндпоинт)
Get device connection status (works for both Real and E2B devices)

post.description:
Get presigned URL for direct S3 upload (FAST - recommended)

Request body:
{
    "session_id": "xxx",
    "task_id": "xxx" (optional)
}

Returns:
{
    "upload_url": "https://...",
    "fields": {...},  # Form fields for POST
    "screenshot_id": "uuid",
    "expires_at": "2025-01-20T...",
    "method": "POST"
}

Client should POST multipart/form-data to upload_url with fields + file

Отменяет выполнение задачи агента по session_id.

Работает в distributed environment - задача может выполняться на любом инстансе Agent.
Опционально уведомляет устройство через WebSocket.

Доступно пользователям для отмены их собственных задач.

get.description:
Получает статус задачи агента (активна ли, отменена ли).

Доступно пользователям для проверки статуса их задач.

Get the list of all available MCP tools

Args:
    user_id: User ID for tool availability check (auth check)

Returns tools in Claude/OpenAI function calling format:
- name: tool name
- description: what it does
- input_schema: JSON Schema of parameters

Usage in the agent planner:
```python
# GET /agent/mcp/tools?user_id=...
tools = response['tools']

# Pass to LLM
llm_response = anthropic.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022",
    tools=tools,
    messages=[...]
)
```

Get Custom MCP server configs for direct Agent connection.

Returns server configs (URL, transport, headers) for each user's
custom_mcp connector with status=connected. Agent uses these to
establish MCP sessions and discover tools at runtime.

post.description:
Receive async audit log of Composio tool usage from the Agent.

The Agent connects directly to Composio MCP Server (bypassing Backend)
for tool execution. This endpoint gives Backend visibility into which
tools are used, how often, and with what results — without blocking
the agent's tool call flow.

Returns 200 immediately. Never blocks.

get.description:
Get Composio MCP server config for direct Agent connection.

Returns {url, headers, entity_id, disabled_toolkits} that the Agent uses
to connect to the Composio MCP Server directly (bypassing Backend for
tool execution).

The returned MCP session is scoped to only the toolkits the user has
enabled.  Toolkits where the user set ``is_active=False`` are excluded
via Composio's session-level toolkit filtering.

Args:
    user_id: User ID whose tools to access.
    toolkits: Optional comma-separated list of toolkit slugs to enable
        (explicit allow-list). When provided, only these toolkits are
        exposed.  If omitted, all connected toolkits are exposed except
        those the user has disabled (``is_active=False``).

Check MCP integrations health

Shows which integrations are available (both legacy providers and Composio)

Get list of all available MCP integrations with connection status.

Returns Composio toolkits + Telegram as a legacy/custom provider.

Get list of user's connected integrations.

Includes Telegram (custom) and Composio-managed connections.
Optionally filter by status.

schema.title: Response List Connected Integrations Mcp Integrations Connected Get

get.description:
Get OAuth redirect URL for a toolkit without creating a local DB record yet.

Used by the in-chat MCP connect flow: the frontend opens a popup and
navigates it to the returned redirect_url. The actual DB record is
created on successful OAuth callback (handled by Composio webhook).

post.description:
Disconnect an integration.

Routes to Composio for managed providers.
Telegram uses its dedicated disconnect endpoint.

Args:
    provider: Provider/toolkit name
    body: Optional JSON body with ``account_id`` for multi-account disconnect

patch.description:
Update the account_label on a specific ComposioConnection.

Only the owning user can update their connection label.
The label is a user-friendly name like "Work Gmail" or "Personal Gmail".

Toggle the is_active flag on a user's Composio connection.

When is_active=False the connection is preserved (Composio account stays
connected) but the toolkit's tools are hidden from the agent.

If ``account_id`` is provided in the body, toggles only that specific
account. Otherwise toggles all accounts for the toolkit.

post.description:
Initiate Telegram user account connection.

Steps:
1. User provides phone number
2. System sends verification code to phone
3. User calls /verify with code
4. Session saved to database

Note: This is NOT OAuth. Telegram app credentials (api_id/api_hash) are configured server-side.

Verify Telegram connection with phone code.

After calling /connect, user receives code on phone.
Call this endpoint with the code to complete connection.

delete.description: Disconnect Telegram account

get.description: Get Telegram connection status

post.description:
Reconnect Telegram account after session was revoked.

This endpoint automatically uses stored credentials (api_id, api_hash, phone)
and initiates a new authorization flow.

get.description:
List all toolkit categories with the number of toolkits in each.

Useful for sidebar rendering: the frontend can hide categories
with ``count == 0`` instead of showing "No connectors found".

Search available Composio toolkits with pagination.

Returns a paginated list of toolkits matching the query and/or category filter.
When no filters are provided, returns all available toolkits.

Get details for a specific toolkit by slug.

post.description:
Receive and process Composio webhook events.

Verifies HMAC signature using COMPOSIO_WEBHOOK_SECRET.
Extracts event details and forwards to agent via Socket.IO.

This endpoint is publicly accessible (no auth) but HMAC-protected.

get.description:
List all apps that support automations (have triggers), with counts.

GET /mcp/triggers/apps
Returns: [{slug, name, trigger_count}, ...]

List user's active triggers with their automation instructions.

post.description:
Create a new event trigger.

The trigger will fire when the specified event occurs in the connected toolkit,
sending a notification to the agent via Socket.IO.

Include an `action_prompt` to tell the agent what to do when triggered
(e.g., "Create a task in Linear with the email subject as title").

patch.description:
Update a trigger's configuration.

Can update the automation instruction (action_prompt) and/or
enable/disable the trigger (is_active).

Shows each time the trigger fired, what the agent decided,

post.description:
Connect to a Cloud PC.

If cloud_pc_id is provided, reconnects to that instance.
Otherwise creates a new one (respecting max_cloud_pcs limit).

Graceful disconnect: backup to S3 + pause sandbox.

The Cloud PC state is preserved for up to 30 days.
Billing reconciliation is handled inside svc.disconnect().

get.description: List all Cloud PCs for the authenticated user.
Get current Cloud PC status for the authenticated user.

post.description:
Keep-alive heartbeat. Call every 60s while Cloud PC is active.

Prevents auto-pause due to inactivity.
Deducts 1 token per minute for Cloud PC usage.

Body (optional):
    is_active: true if user had mouse/keyboard activity since last heartbeat.
    cloud_pc_id: specific Cloud PC instance ID.

post.description: Download a file from the Cloud PC via presigned S3 URL.
Download a previously published file from S3 (no active sandbox needed).
Copy an uploaded attachment from S3 into the Cloud PC sandbox at /home/user/output/.
Get a presigned S3 URL for uploading a file to the Cloud PC.
Callback after file is uploaded to S3 — moves it into the Cloud PC sandbox.
Create a directory on the Cloud PC.
Link Cloud PC as a device for AI agent access in chat.
Unlink Cloud PC from device (removes from device picker in chat).

User takes control of the Cloud PC desktop.

Sends a pause signal to the running agent, switches desktop control to user.

Resume the AI agent after user had control of the Cloud PC desktop.

Drains queued messages, constructs resume context, and creates a new
RabbitMQ task so the agent picks up where it left off.
Renew user's Cloud PC control TTL. Called every 50s by frontend.

Soft-delete a Cloud PC.

Kills the E2B sandbox to save compute, but preserves the CloudPC record
(with deleted_at set) and S3 backup for 30-day recovery.

Agent requests user confirmation for action (via Redis, fast).

Flow:
1. Agent calls this → saved to Redis with TTL
2. User polls /pending → sees confirmation
3. User approves/rejects → agent gets WebSocket notification

User polls this endpoint every 2-5 seconds.

post.description:
User responds to confirmation (approve/reject).
Updates Redis and notifies agent via WebSocket immediately.

post.description:
Track a client-side analytics event.

This endpoint allows authenticated clients to send analytics events
that are then forwarded to configured analytics platforms (GA4, Yandex Metrica, Amplitude).

**Allowed events:**
- `app_installed`: User downloaded and installed the application
- `app_launched`: User launched the application
- `app_load_time`: App startup performance metric
- `app_crash`: Application crash event
- `app_uninstalled`: User uninstalled the application
- `session_started`: User started a new chat session
- `session_ended`: User ended a chat session
- `daily_active_user`: DAU tracking (sent once per day)
- `monthly_active_user`: MAU tracking (sent once per month)
- `device_installed`: Device registration with breakdown by platform/device_type

**Note:** Events like `registration_completed` and `first_task_completed`
are tracked automatically by the backend and cannot be sent via this endpoint.
The `device_installed` event is also tracked automatically when POST /devices is called.

Track an application crash event.

This endpoint is specifically designed for crash reporting with additional
metadata like error message, stack trace, and app version.

Track application load time for performance monitoring.

This endpoint tracks app startup performance metrics.

Track chat session start.

This endpoint tracks when a user starts a new chat session.

Track chat session end.

This endpoint tracks when a user ends a chat session with duration and message count.

Track app uninstallation.

This endpoint tracks when a user uninstalls the app and triggers
the Resend email campaign for churned users.

get.description:
Get analytics service status.

Returns information about which analytics platforms are enabled.

post.description:
[ADMIN] Trigger the inactive users detection job.

This job:
1. Finds users who haven't been active for N days
2. Tracks them in analytics (Amplitude, GA4, Yandex)
3. Sends re-engagement emails via Resend

[ADMIN] Trigger the retention tracking job.

This job tracks user retention on specific days after signup.
Default retention days: 1, 7, 14, 30

Returns daily and monthly active user counts.

Shows retention rates for different cohorts.

[ADMIN] Get current analytics configuration.

Returns configured analytics providers and settings.
Get pricing information (no auth required).

Internal endpoint for Agent to check a user's billing access.

Authenticated via the shared agent token (Authorization: Bearer or
X-Agent-Token header).  Returns the same BillingStatusResponse as
GET /billing/status but for an arbitrary user_id — so the Agent can
check the *user's* billing rather than its own.

Get user's billing status.

Returns whether user can access the service and why.

Frontend should use the client_secret with Stripe.js to collect card details.
After successful card setup, call POST /billing/confirm-card.

get.description: Get token transactions grouped by chat session.

post.description:
Two-phase card setup in a single endpoint.

**Phase 1** — create SetupIntent (no setup_intent_id in body):
  Returns `{"step": "confirm_card", "client_secret": "...", "setup_intent_id": "..."}`
  Frontend uses client_secret with Stripe.js to collect the card.

**Phase 2** — confirm card (supply setup_intent_id):
  Returns `{"step": "done", "has_payment_method": true}`

Error codes:
  stripe_unavailable   — Stripe is not configured
  setup_not_complete   — SetupIntent status is not "succeeded"
  missing_customer     — customer/payment_method missing on SetupIntent
  stripe_error         — unexpected Stripe error

Unified subscribe / change-plan endpoint.

Automatically detects the user's current state:
- No active Stripe subscription → creates a new subscription
- Active subscription on a different plan → changes the plan (with proration)
- Already on the requested plan → returns error plan_already_active

Error codes:
  stripe_unavailable   — Stripe is not configured
  plan_not_found       — requested plan_id does not exist or has no Stripe price
  no_payment_method    — user has no valid card on file
  plan_already_active  — user is already on this plan
  payment_pending      — plan change initiated but payment not yet confirmed
  stripe_error         — unexpected Stripe error

Handle Stripe webhook events.

This endpoint receives events from Stripe for:
- Subscription created/updated/deleted
- Invoice paid/failed
- Checkout completed

Stripe sends events as POST requests with a signature header.

get.description: Health check for webhook endpoint.

Get unread notifications for the current user.

Returns notifications that haven't been marked as read yet.
Get all notifications for the current user (read and unread).

Get count of unread notifications.

Lightweight endpoint for polling or badge display.

post.description: Mark a specific notification as read.
Mark all notifications as read for the current user.

Internal endpoint for worker to send notifications to user via Socket.IO.

This endpoint is called by the worker service (which runs in a separate container)
to send real-time notifications to connected users.

Supported notification types:
- agent_response: Final response from agent
- agent_error: Error from agent
- agent_status_update: Agent status change
- agent_step: Intermediate step from agent
- task_progress: Task execution progress
- new_message: New chat message
